methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Impulse Labs is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Sam D'Amico that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 private companies at the same stage and sector, supplemented by reference to 1 public comparable and additional private frontier-tech participants in climate and energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $50M. Peer-median analysis references four same-stage, same-sector comparables: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). The median valuation across this peer set is $175M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B reflecting meaningful dispersion—primarily driven by Watershed's significant outlier position.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $42M (floor) to $201M (ceiling), with a median point of $112M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate multiples in frontier-energy technology and the outsized valuation of top-decile peers like Watershed. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the peer median given high dispersion, and whether Impulse Labs' specific technology trajectory and market position justify positioning toward the median or higher end of the range. Additional diligence on revenue trajectory, capital efficiency, and competitive positioning would narrow this range.
